summary: Fire‑blazing Scarlet>Fire, soaring Estimated Prophet and epic Other One
blurb: The second set delivers a scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, a towering Estimated Prophet jam that rolls into Eyes of the World, and an unstoppable Other One. Jerry’s voice holds steady while Brent shines on Take You Home. Overall a solid New Orleans night with several high‑water marks.
review: On October 18, 1988 at Keifer Lakefront Arena, the Dead turned New Orleans into a playground for their late‑80s wizardry. The first set rolls along nicely, but it’s the second that truly ignites: a seamless Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition erupts into one of those classic “Scarlet>Fire” moments that still get fans buzzing. From there the band lifts off with an expansive Estimated Prophet that morphs into an uplifting Eyes Of The World, giving Mickey and Bill a chance to go "bananas" during the thunder‑calling passage. The highlight reel continues deep into the night as Brent Mydland drives a killer Take You Home straight into an epic Other One, keeping the energy high until the final encore of Iko Iko and Knockin’ On Heaven’s Door. Jerry’s vocals sound solid despite recent laryngitis, his guitar solos are mesmerizing when they click, and Bob’s steady vocal presence helps balance the mix even if it leans heavy at times. Brent’s keyboard work shines throughout, especially on Take You Home, though a few matrix mixes bury his organ a bit. All told, this show captures the band in a creative groove with enough peaks to make it worth a spin for any Deadhead looking for that late‑80s sparkle.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings describe the show as mixed, but a third is clearly positive; multiple reviewers praise the Scarlet>Fire sequence, Estimated Prophet jam, and Other One while noting only occasional uneven moments. The overall tone leans positively across the analyses.

best_recording.reason: Clean, well‑mixed soundboard source that captures the key highlights (Estimated Prophet, Bird Song, Scarlet>Fire) without major dropouts or muddiness.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence ignites the second set
Soaring Estimated Prophet jam that flows into Eyes Of The World
Epic Other One linked directly from Take You Home, showcasing Brent’s keyboards
Solid vocal performance by Jerry despite recent laryngitis

band_performance.Jerry: Mesmerizing solo work when it clicks, solid vocal delivery despite recent laryngitis; some moments felt less inspirational.
band_performance.Bob: Steady vocal presence that helped balance the mix, though his voice was occasionally heavy in the matrix mixes.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard work shines, especially on Take You Home where he 'kills' the song; occasional low mix level noted in matrix recordings.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums drive the band forward, particularly during the thunder‑calling passage of Estimated Prophet.
band_performance.Billy: Shares the high‑energy drumming with Mickey, adding to the powerful rhythmic foundation throughout the set.
